eligible_applicant_type: Principal cities of Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSAs)
Other metropolitan cities with populations of at least 50,000
Qualified Urban Counties with populations of at least 200,000 (excluding the population of entitled cities)
Non-entitlement Units of General Local Government (UGLG)
project_area: Community development, housing, economic opportunities, and suitable living environment for low- and moderate-income persons
demographic_requirements: At least 70% of CDBG funds must benefit low- and moderate-income persons over a 1, 2, or 3-year period.
eligibility_summary: Eligible applicants include principal cities of MSAs, metropolitan cities with populations ≥50,000, urban counties with populations ≥200,000, states, insular areas, and non-entitlement UGLGs. Funds must address national objectives such as benefiting low- and moderate-income persons, preventing/eliminating slums or blight, or addressing urgent community needs. HUD does not provide funds directly to individuals or non-governmental entities; applicants must contact local municipal or county officials.
